ramayanaStories.title: Baby Rama
ramayanaStories.story: Long ago, in the kingdom of Ayodhya, King Dasharatha had four sons. The eldest was Rama. Baby Rama was very special. He had big lotus eyes and a sweet smile. Everyone loved to see baby Rama crawl and play. When he was a little boy, he could break the great bow of Lord Shiva! Even as a child, Rama was brave and kind. He always listened to his parents and teachers.
ramayanaStories.lesson: Always respect your elders and be kind to everyone! 🙏

ramayanaStories.title: Rama Goes to the Forest
ramayanaStories.story: When Rama was young, his father's wife Kaikeyi asked the king to send Rama to the forest for 14 years. Rama was very sad but he kept his promise. His wife Sita and brother Lakshmana went with him. In the forest, they lived simply. They wore clothes made of bark and ate fruits and roots. Rama never complained. He was always brave and happy.
ramayanaStories.lesson: Keep your promises no matter how difficult! 💪

ramayanaStories.title: Hanuman Meets Rama
ramayanaStories.story: One day, Rama met a great devotee named Hanuman. Hanuman was a vanara (monkey) with great strength. When Hanuman met Rama, he bowed down and said, 'I am your servant, my Lord!' Rama hugged Hanuman. From that day, Hanuman became Rama's best friend and helper. Hanuman always helped Rama with his great strength and devotion.
ramayanaStories.lesson: True friendship and devotion can overcome any obstacle! 🤝

ramayanaStories.title: Sita in the Garden
ramayanaStories.story: When Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana lived in the forest, they had a beautiful hut near a river. Sita loved the flowers in the forest. One day, a golden deer came near their hut. Sita liked it very much and asked Rama to catch it for her. The golden deer was actually a demon in disguise. This was the beginning of many troubles, but Sita remained brave and faithful.
ramayanaStories.lesson: Do not be fooled by beautiful appearances! 🦌

ramayanaStories.title: Hanuman Flies to Lanka
ramayanaStories.story: When the demon king Ravana took Sita to Lanka, Rama needed to find her. Hanuman said, 'I will find Mother Sita!' Hanuman grew very big and jumped across the ocean. He flew to Lanka and found Sita sitting under a tree. Hanuman gave her Rama's ring as a sign. Sita was happy to hear about Rama. Hanuman then burned Lanka with his tail and returned to Rama with the good news!
ramayanaStories.lesson: Devotion and courage can achieve the impossible! 🔥

ramayanaStories.title: The Bridge to Lanka
ramayanaStories.story: Rama needed to reach Lanka to rescue Sita. The ocean was very wide. Rama prayed to the ocean god. Then, all the vanaras (monkeys) and bears started building a bridge! They wrote Rama's name on stones and threw them into the ocean. Amazingly, the stones floated! Soon, a beautiful bridge was ready. Rama and his army crossed the bridge to Lanka.
ramayanaStories.lesson: When everyone works together, great things happen! 🌉

ramayanaStories.title: Rama Defeats Ravana
ramayanaStories.story: Ravana was a powerful demon king with ten heads. He had taken Sita to Lanka. Rama fought a great battle with Ravana. Ravana was very strong, but Rama was stronger because he was good and righteous. Rama prayed to Goddess Durga and shot a powerful arrow. The arrow hit Ravana and he fell down. Good had won over evil! Everyone cheered for Rama.
ramayanaStories.lesson: Good always wins over evil! Be brave and do good! ⚔️

ramayanaStories.title: Rama Returns to Ayodhya
ramayanaStories.story: After 14 years, Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana returned to Ayodhya. The people of Ayodhya were very happy! They lit thousands of oil lamps to welcome Rama. The whole city was decorated with lights and flowers. This day is celebrated as Diwali - the festival of lights! Rama became the king and everyone lived happily ever after.
ramayanaStories.lesson: Patience and goodness are always rewarded! 🪔

ramayanaStories.title: Jatayu the Brave Bird
ramayanaStories.story: When Ravana was taking Sita to Lanka, a brave old bird named Jatayu tried to save her. Jatayu was a great warrior in his youth. Even though he was old, he fought bravely with Ravana. Ravana cut his wings, but Jatayu did not give up. He fell down but remembered everything. Later, Rama found Jatayu and gave him peace. Jatayu is remembered as a great hero!
ramayanaStories.lesson: Bravery is not about age or strength, but about courage in heart! 🦅

ramayanaStories.title: The Loyal Lakshmana
ramayanaStories.story: Lakshmana was Rama's younger brother. He loved Rama very much. When Rama went to the forest, Lakshmana went with him. For 14 years, Lakshmana served Rama and Sita. He never slept at night and guarded them. When Sita was in trouble, Lakshmana drew a line around the hut and told her not to cross it. Lakshmana is remembered for his loyalty and devotion.
ramayanaStories.lesson: Love and loyalty to family is the greatest virtue! 💝

ramayanaStories.title: Sita's Trial by Fire
ramayanaStories.story: After rescuing Sita from Ravana, some people doubted Sita's purity. To prove her innocence, Sita prayed to Agni, the fire god, and entered a magical fire. The fire did not burn her! Instead, the fire god himself appeared and declared Sita pure and innocent. Rama and everyone were relieved!
ramayanaStories.lesson: The truth always shines through! Honesty and purity are invincible! 🔥

ramayanaStories.title: Bharata's Love for Rama
ramayanaStories.story: When Bharata learned that his mother had sent Rama to the forest, he was very angry. He went to the forest to bring Rama back, but Rama refused. Bharata took Rama's sandals and placed them on the throne! He ruled as Rama's regent for 14 years, always caring for the people as Rama would have.
ramayanaStories.lesson: True love means putting others first! Brotherhood is sacred! 👑

ramayanaStories.title: Shabari's Devotion
ramayanaStories.story: In the forest, Rama met an old woman named Shabari. She was a devoted servant of Rama for many years. Every day she would collect berries for Rama. Before eating each berry, she would taste it to make sure it was sweet. When Rama ate her berries, he said, 'No one has ever given me such sweet food!'
ramayanaStories.lesson: True devotion comes from the heart! Love matters more than perfection! 🍇

ramayanaStories.title: Sugriva and Vali
ramayanaStories.story: Sugriva and Vali were brothers and Vanara kings. Once, Vali fought a demon and Sugriva thought he was dead. Sugriva became king. But Vali returned alive and angry. He banished Sugriva. When Rama met Sugriva, he promised to help. Rama killed Vali from hiding, and Sugriva became king. Sugriva then helped Rama find Sita.
ramayanaStories.lesson: Sometimes helping one person leads to great victories! 🤝
